title: Intermodel Relationships
text: Intermodel Relationships The names of modeled entities have the format Entity.Model , in which the suffix after the dot is the Model Name property. This is a mandatory property that defines a namespace for entity. All entities with the same model name are said to be part of the same application model. Inter-model relationships can exist between entities in different application models. Inter-model relationships behave as though they are owned by the Many entity. This means that when you carry out an operation on the Many entity, this also directly affects the inter-model relationship. You cannot delete the One-entity for which an inter-model relationship exists. To delete the relationship, delete the Many entity. When creating inter-model relationships: Define inter-model relationships only when referential integrity is handled by Uniface. You cannot use the Create Script utility for inter-model relationships. Limit the number of inter-model relationships. If you export or import modeled entities involved in an inter-model relationship, ensure that both sides of the relationship are included. If not, the resulting relationship will be incomplete. Related Links Referential Integrity Referential integrity is a standard feature of relational database design that prevents users or applications from entering inconsistent data. Most relational databases have referential integrity rules that you can apply when you create a relationship between tables. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
